lastPortSummary: ~$28–30K AUM spread across 20 positions is over-diversified, diluting conviction and meaningful alpha. Tech concentration is ~65–70% of portfolio — not a value portfolio, it's a growth portfolio with value labels. No entry prices were provided, creating a significant gap in assessing P&L, cost basis risk, or sizing discipline. CSPX and QQQM overlap with nearly every equity holding, creating redundant passive exposure alongside active stock risk. S63 is the only true value/dividend position. The portfolio carries zero fixed income, zero commodities, and zero defensive yield — no ballast for a short-term horizon.

intelFeed.text:
## 🔴 URGENT ACTIONS (do today)

**TSLA → TRIM → $390–$420 zone → Elon distraction risk, auto margin compression, and Q1 delivery miss narrative make this rally a gift to reduce exposure before it reverses.**

**ADBE → TRIM → Any print near $250–$260 → Structural AI disruption to core Creative Cloud is accelerating; this is a dead-cat bounce window, not a recovery.**

**PLTR → MONITOR STOP → Watch $118 closely today → If PLTR opens or closes below $118, the short-term consolidation thesis breaks and the trim becomes a forced exit.**

---

## 📋 PORTFOLIO REVIEW (positions to act on this week)

### PLTR — TRIM (Short-Term Tranche)
- **Action:** Trim short-term tranche on any print at **$145–$155**
- **Kill Switch:** Close below **$118** → exit remaining short-term allocation immediately
- **Long-term floor:** Below **$90** the position becomes uninvestable on any value framework
- **This week:** PLTR has been riding AI/defense sentiment. With tariff uncertainty weighing on government IT spend timelines and no near-term catalyst to push through $155, the risk/reward tilts to trimming into any strength. Do not chase. If it runs to $145+, sell the tranche. If it fades, the stop at $118 does the work.
- **Market conditions support trimming:** Yes. Rotation out of high-multiple tech is ongoing. PLTR at ~100x forward earnings has zero margin of safety. Reduce now.

---

### TSLA — TRIM (with full exit optionality)
- **Action:** Trim at **$390–$420**; full exit trigger on break of **$340**
- **Kill Switch:** Close below **$310** → exit all remaining
- **This week:** TSLA is navigating a perfect storm of headwinds — Q1 deliveries disappointed, Cybertruck recall noise, EU/China competitive pressure from BYD, and Musk's political capital being spent on DOGE rather than product. Any bounce toward $390–$420 is a distribution zone, not a re-entry. Use it.
- **Market conditions support trimming:** Yes. At $400, TSLA is pricing in a recovery that the fundamentals don't yet support. Trim aggressively in this range.

---

### ADBE — TRIM (Structural Exit)
- **Action:** Trim on any bounce to **$250–$260**; accelerate full exit below **$220**
- **Kill Switch:** Below **$220** → do not wait, exit the position
- **This week:** ADBE faces an existential question: can it monetise AI fast enough to offset the creative workflow disruption from Firefly competitors, OpenAI Sora, and Midjourney? The market is saying no. The failed Figma acquisition removed a defensive moat acquisition at exactly the wrong time. Any bounce here is technical, not fundamental.
- **Market conditions support trimming:** Yes. Enterprise software is under pressure from AI substitution narratives. ADBE has no near-term catalyst. Trim into any strength this week.

---

### MA — HOLD (No change)
- **Conditions to watch:** At ~$521, MA is ~35x forward earnings — not a value entry but a quality hold. No stop specified. Monitor for macro deterioration (consumer spending slowdown, credit delinquency uptick) as the primary risk to thesis.
- **No action required this week** unless MA breaks below $480 on volume, which would warrant reassessment.

---

## 🔭 WATCHLIST UPDATES (scanner stocks — ready to enter?)

**CVX — WATCH, approaching entry zone.** Chevron has pulled back toward the $150–$158 range on oil price weakness (Brent ~$73–$75). Entry zone is **$145–$158** — you are near the top of the zone. Do not buy the full position yet; wait for confirmation that oil stabilises or CVX holds $148 on a weekly close. A break below $145 delays entry.

**BRK.B — NOT YET, above entry zone.** BRK.B is trading above the **$435–$455** entry target. Berkshire's cash fortress (~$330B+) is the ultimate value anchor but you are not getting the price you need. Keep watching — a broader market sell-off (SPX -8 to -12%) would likely bring BRK.B into range. Patience required. Do not chase above $455.

**GOOG — WATCH CLOSELY, may be entering the zone.** Alphabet has traded down materially on AI competition fears (OpenAI SearchGPT, Perplexity) and regulatory overhang. If GOOG is trading in the **$155–$168** range this week, this is your highest-conviction near-term entry. Q1 earnings are a near-term catalyst — watch for Search revenue resilience and Cloud growth. If earnings print well and stock holds $155+, this is actionable. **Highest priority watchlist entry this week.**

**D05 (DBS Group, SGX) — WATCH, valuation-dependent.** DBS remains Singapore's premier banking franchise. At current SGX prices (~SGD 38–42 range, verify today's print), the stock trades at ~1.3–1.5x book with an ~5–6% dividend yield. Entry becomes compelling on a pullback toward **SGD 37–39** — this is where the dividend yield approaches 6%+ and the margin of safety improves. A Fed pivot or Singapore rate normalisation is a medium-term tailwind. Monitor SGD/USD FX if you are a USD-based investor — currency drag is a real consideration.

---

## 📡 MARKET INTELLIGENCE (macro signals affecting your positions)

**1. Macro Signal — "Higher for Longer" Re-rating Tech Duration:**
The Fed's refusal to cut at 4.25–4.50% is the single most important variable for your portfolio today. Your three TRIM candidates (PLTR, TSLA, ADBE) are all long-duration, high-multiple assets — the assets most punished when the discount rate stays elevated. Every week without a Fed pivot is another week these valuations face headwinds. The rotation into value (CVX, BRK.B, financials like MA and DBS) is not temporary noise — it is a structural repricing driven by cost of capital. This validates your TRIM thesis and your watchlist construction simultaneously.

**2. Key Risk — A TSLA or PLTR Earnings Surprise Traps You:**
Both TSLA and PLTR have earnings in the near-term window (TSLA Q1 results expected late April 2026; PLTR reports in May). If either company surprises to the upside — a government contract win for PLTR or a margins beat from TSLA — the stock could gap up 15–20% overnight, making your trim targets moot and potentially reversing momentum. **This is why you must trim into current strength, not wait for the perfect price.** Missing the top by 5% is acceptable. Being caught in a reversal below your stops is not.

**3. Opportunity — GOOG Pre-Earnings Entry Window:**
Alphabet's Q1 2026 earnings represent a binary catalyst. If Search revenue holds and Google Cloud continues its growth trajectory (which competitive dynamics suggest is likely despite AI noise), GOOG could re-rate 10–15% from current levels. At **$155–$168**, you are being paid to take the risk — the market is pricing in structural Search decline that has not yet materialised in the revenue data. This is a classic value investor's entry: fear-based selling of a durable franchise. If GOOG is in your entry zone this week, initiate a starter position (25–30% of intended allocation) before earnings, and add on a confirmed strong print.

---

## ✅ TODAY'S ACTION CHECKLIST

1. **TSLA** — Place a limit sell order for your trim tranche at **$410** (mid-range of $390–$420); if TSLA opens above $390 today, execute the trim before end of session.
2. **ADBE** — Set a limit sell order at **$255** for your trim tranche; if not filled by Friday, reassess — do not let the week pass without reducing ADBE exposure.
3. **PLTR** — Set a price alert at **$118**; if PLTR closes at or below $118 today or any day this week,

## 🔴 URGENT ACTIONS (do today)

- **NFLX** → **TRIM 4–5 shares** → at market open (~$95–$100 range) → Post-earnings/guidance drop of -9.72% breaks momentum; advertising tier growth concerns make further downside likely before stabilization.
- **TSLA** → **CHECK vs $340 stop** → If premarket shows continued weakness below $350, prepare limit sell on 1–2 shares at $420–$430 on any relief bounce → Musk brand/political overhang + EV margin compression = asymmetric downside risk at current levels.
- **SNDK** → **SET LIMIT TRIM ORDER** → Place GTC limit sell (30–40% of position) at $950–$960 → Largest position, +37% unrealized gain; locking in partial profits is portfolio hygiene, not a thesis call.

---

## 📋 PORTFOLIO REVIEW (positions to act on this week)

**SNDK — TRIM (High Priority)**
- Action: Trim 30–40% of position at **$950–$980**
- Kill switch: Close below **$820**
- Market context today: Storage/memory sector remains volatile amid tariff uncertainty on semiconductor imports (US-China friction). A 90-day tariff pause gives a window, but memory names are first to reprice on demand shocks. *Conditions support executing the trim this week, not waiting.* The gain is real; concentration risk is real. Set the GTC limit now.

**TSLA — TRIM (High Priority)**
- Action: Sell 1–2 shares at **$420–$430** on next bounce
- Kill switch: Close below **$340** → full reassessment
- Market context today: TSLA faces compounding headwinds — Musk political fatigue, Q1 delivery miss, EV price war with BYD, and macro consumer softness. No obvious near-term catalyst to push toward $430. *Advice unchanged; if no bounce materializes within 2 weeks, revisit position sizing downward regardless of price.* The $164 unrealized gain on your basis deserves protection.

**NKE — TRIM (Moderate Priority)**
- Action: Exit 15–20 shares if **no catalyst above $50 within 60 days** (clock starts now)
- Kill switch: Close below **$40** → full exit
- Market context today: Nike is caught in a consumer discretionary squeeze — higher-for-longer rates pressure aspirational spending, tariffs on Vietnam/China manufacturing raise input costs, and brand momentum has stalled. Flat at $46 with 34.7 shares is dead capital. *60-day clock is running. Begin mentally treating $40 as the hard floor.* No new information today changes this — but tariff escalation risk makes the downside scenario more plausible.

**NFLX — TRIM (Urgent)**
- Action: Trim **4–5 shares** at market open or first available liquidity
- Kill switch: Close below **$85** → full exit
- Market context today: The -9.72% drop is a signal, not noise. Whether driven by guidance disappointment or slowing ad-tier monetization, the market has re-rated the stock. At $97.31 with 12.69 shares, you hold ~$1,235 in a name that just broke down. *Trim to reduce exposure; do not average down. Wait for price stabilization before reassessing the remainder.*

---

## 🔭 WATCHLIST UPDATES (scanner stocks — ready to enter?)

**CVX — entry zone $140–$152 | Target $195–$210**
⚠️ *Approaching actionable.* Chevron has been pressured by oil's slide toward the low-$60s (WTI), which could bring CVX into the $140–$148 zone imminently. Watch for a weekly close above $148 with oil stabilization as your trigger. Not yet a full entry — wait for price confirmation. Set a price alert at **$148**.

**BRK.B — entry zone $445–$465 | Target $580–$620**
👀 *Not yet in zone.* BRK.B trades closer to $480–$500 range currently. No entry yet. Berkshire's cash fortress (~$330B+) and insurance earnings make it a gift if macro deteriorates further. Keep watching — a broader market selloff (S&P -5% to -8% from here) would bring it into range. *Best buy-the-dip candidate in the watchlist.*

**LYB (LyondellBasell) — entry zone $66–$74 | Target $105–$115**
🟡 *Potentially in or near zone.* LYB has been beaten down by petrochemical margin compression and China demand concerns. Dividend yield above 8% at these levels provides a floor argument. *Check current price — if trading $68–$74, this is an actionable value entry for the long-term sleeve.* High tariff sensitivity is the key risk; size small initially.

**D05.SI (DBS Group) — entry zone SGD 38.50–40.50**
👀 *Monitor closely.* Singapore banks have benefited from high rates but face NIM compression risk as MAS signals potential easing. DBS at SGD ~38–40 would represent fair value for a best-in-class ASEAN franchise. *If SGX opens Monday near SGD 39.00–39.50, this enters the actionable zone.* Catalyst watch: next MAS policy statement and DBS Q1 earnings.

---

## 📡 MARKET INTELLIGENCE (macro signals affecting your positions)

**(1) Macro Signal — Higher-for-Longer Biting Consumer Names**
The Fed's hold at 4.25–4.50% is increasingly visible in consumer discretionary stress. This directly pressures **NKE** (aspirational spending softness), **NFLX** (subscription churn risk if ad-tier disappoints), and **TSLA** (auto financing costs suppress EV demand). Your three TRIM candidates are all exposed to this same macro thread. The signal reinforces urgency on all three.

**(2) Key Risk — Tariff Escalation Triggering Stop-Losses**
The 90-day tariff pause expires, and any re-escalation targeting semiconductors, consumer electronics, or apparel would hit **SNDK**, **NVDA**, **AMD**, **NKE**, and **ASML** simultaneously. A shock announcement over a weekend (a known pattern with this administration) could gap your stops. **SNDK's $820 kill switch and NKE's $40 kill switch are most at risk in a tariff shock scenario.** Consider whether your stop on SNDK should be a mental stop with a limit order placed, not a hard stop-loss, to avoid a gap fill exit at a bad price.

**(3) Opportunity — ASML Remains a BUY MORE; CVX Entry Window Opening**
Your existing BUY MORE on **ASML** is validated: EUV monopoly, TSMC capex discipline, and European defense/industrial rotation make this a rare quality compounder on sale. If ASML pulls back toward the $680–$720 range on macro noise, that is the add trigger. Separately, **CVX approaching $140–$148** this week creates a high-conviction value entry opportunity — FCF yield, Permian Basin optionality, and dividend safety (4%+ yield) make it your best near-term watchlist-to-portfolio conversion candidate.

US: The 10-year Treasury yield has stabilized around 4.3–4.5% following the Fed's patient hold stance, compressing growth multiples further but creating a favorable entry window for deep-value industrials and financials trading at sub-12x forward P/E. Watch for Q1 earnings beats in regional banks — net interest margin resilience is surprising to the upside.
SGX/SEA: Singapore's MAS held policy steady with a slight SGD NEER slope reduction, signaling tolerance for modest easing. This benefits S-REITs and dividend-heavy blue chips (banks, Jardine group) as local rate expectations soften. STI remains undervalued vs. regional peers at ~11x P/E with 4.5%+ dividend yield support.

**2. ACTIONABLE TRIGGER**
**Entry:** DBS Group (D05.SI) — accumulate on any pullback toward SGD 38.50–39.00. Q1 2026 results expected late April; dividend yield at current levels exceeds 5.5%. The MAS easing tilt removes a key headwind. Stop below SGD 37.80.
US pairing: Add to beaten-down energy names (XOM, CVX) if WTI holds above $78/bbl post-inventory data Wednesday — both trade at FCF yields above 8%.

**3. RISK TO WATCH THIS WEEK**
US-China trade escalation rhetoric intensifying ahead of the 90-day tariff pause expiry window. Any breakdown signal — particularly export controls on semiconductors or retaliatory measures on US services — could trigger a sharp risk-off move in SGX tech-adjacent names (Venture Corp, UMS) and US small-cap industrials with China supply chain exposure. Monitor Thursday's White House trade briefings closely.
